contraindications.clinicalRationale: Diuretics can precipitate acute kidney injury (AKI) and electrolyte abnormalities. There is concern for volume depletion and AKI, particularly among patients treated concurrently with diuretics or who have tenuous volume status.
contraindications.riskDescription: Acute kidney injury, electrolyte abnormalities, volume depletion
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Consider decreasing thiazide or loop diuretic dosages before commencement of SGLT2i treatment
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Diuretics can precipitate acute kidney injury (AKI) and electrolyte abnormalities, and the hypokalemic response to diuretics is diminished with low eGFR

contraindications.clinicalRationale: Salt substitutes containing potassium chloride should be used with caution in patients with advanced CKD or those taking potassium-sparing diuretics, as they may contribute to hyperkalemia.
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Salt substitutes containing potassium chloride should be used with caution in patients with advanced CKD or those taking potassium-sparing diuretics, as they may contribute to hyperkalemia.

contraindications.clinicalRationale: The hypokalemic response to diuretics is diminished with low eGFR and depends on the type of diuretic used. Higher risk of acute kidney injury due to low eGFR with concurrent use of medications that may contribute to kidney hypoperfusion, such as diuretics.
contraindications.riskDescription: Diminished hypokalemic response, increased acute kidney injury risk
contraindications.sourceSnippet: the hypokalemic response to diuretics is diminished with low eGFR and depends on the type of diuretic used

warnings.warningText: Consider decreasing thiazide diuretic dosages before commencement of SGLT2i treatment
warnings.affectedPopulation: Patients at risk for hypovolemia starting SGLT2i
warnings.actionRequired: Monitor volume status, reduce diuretic dose if needed when initiating SGLT2i

warnings.warningText: Consider decreasing loop diuretic dosages before commencement of SGLT2i treatment
warnings.affectedPopulation: Patients at risk for hypovolemia starting SGLT2i
warnings.actionRequired: Monitor volume status, reduce diuretic dose if needed when initiating SGLT2i

warnings.warningText: Monitor serum creatinine and potassium within 2-4 weeks after starting or changing dose when used with ACEi or ARB
warnings.actionRequired: Check serum creatinine and potassium levels within 2-4 weeks, reassess concomitant medications if creatinine increases >30%

warnings.warningText: Diuretics are most compelling for hyperkalemia management when there is concomitant volume overload or hypertension
warnings.actionRequired: Assess for volume overload or hypertension before using diuretics for hyperkalemia management
